Home / Services / Site Grading

Site Grading

Precision site grading for proper drainage and construction readiness.

Proper site grading is crucial for preventing water damage, ensuring structural integrity, and creating a stable foundation for any construction project. DRP Tractor Services provides expert site grading services throughout Albuquerque and the surrounding areas. We utilize advanced equipment and precise techniques to achieve the ideal slope and elevation for your property. Our team understands the importance of managing Albuquerque's monsoon season runoff. Whether you're preparing for a new home build, a commercial development, or simply need to improve drainage on your existing property, we have the expertise and equipment to get the job done right. We work closely with engineers and contractors to ensure that our grading meets all required specifications and regulations. From initial assessment to final compaction, we pay attention to every detail to deliver a smooth, level, and stable surface. We take into account the unique soil conditions prevalent in the Albuquerque area, including sandy soils and caliche layers, to ensure long-lasting results.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site Assessment - We evaluate the existing topography, soil conditions, and drainage patterns.
  2. 02 Step 2: Grading Plan - We develop a detailed grading plan based on your project requirements and local regulations.
  3. 03 Step 3: Rough Grading - We use heavy equipment to move large amounts of soil and establish the general slope.
  4. 04 Step 4: Fine Grading - We refine the surface with precision grading techniques to achieve the desired elevation and compaction.

Benefits

  • Prevents water damage and erosion
  • Ensures a stable foundation for construction
  • Improves drainage and aesthetics
  • Meets all required specifications and regulations

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between rough grading and fine grading?

Rough grading involves moving large amounts of soil to establish the general slope, while fine grading is the precise refinement of the surface to achieve the desired elevation and compaction.

How do you handle drainage issues during site grading?

We carefully analyze the existing drainage patterns and incorporate solutions such as swales, French drains, and proper sloping to effectively manage water runoff.

What equipment do you use for site grading?

We use a variety of heavy equipment, including bulldozers, graders, excavators, and compactors, to efficiently and accurately grade your site.

Get a Free Estimate
